definitions.definition: The branch of optics that deals with the study of the behavior of light as a ray. It involves the study of reflection, refraction, and other phenomena related to the path of light.
definitions.example: The study of the behavior of light as it passes through a prism is an example of ray optics.

definitions.definition: A device that uses light to magnify, measure, or analyze objects, such as microscopes, telescopes, and binoculars.
definitions.example: A microscope is an example of an optical instrument.

definitions.definition: An image that is formed by the actual convergence of light rays at a point in space. It can be projected onto a screen.
definitions.example: A real image is formed when light passes through a convex lens.

definitions.definition: An image that is formed by the apparent divergence of light rays. It cannot be projected onto a screen.
definitions.example: A virtual image is formed when light passes through a concave lens.

definitions.definition: A curved mirror with a spherical surface that reflects light. It can be concave or convex.
definitions.example: A concave mirror is used in solar concentrators.

definitions.definition: A type of spherical mirror that is curved inward. It converges light rays and forms a real image.
definitions.example: A concave mirror is used in telescopes.

definitions.definition: A type of spherical mirror that is curved outward. It diverges light rays and forms a virtual image.
definitions.example: A convex mirror is used in rearview mirrors.

definitions.definition: The distance between the center of a lens or mirror and the point at which it forms an image. It is measured in meters.
definitions.example: The focal length of a convex lens is 10 cm.

definitions.definition: A mathematical equation that relates the object distance, image distance, and focal length of a mirror. It is used to calculate the position of an image.
A mathematical equation that relates the object distance, image distance, and focal length of a lens. It is used to calculate the position of an image.
The ratio of the angle subtended by the image to the angle subtended by the object. It is a measure of the magnifying power of an optical instrument.
definitions.example: The angular magnification of a telescope is 10x.

definitions.definition: The ratio of the height of the image to the height of the object. It is a measure of the magnifying power of an optical instrument.
definitions.example: The linear magnification of a microscope is 10x.

definitions.definition: The center of a mirror or lens. It is the point at which the principal axis intersects the mirror or lens.
definitions.example: The pole of a convex lens is the point at its center.

definitions.definition: The point on the principal axis that is equidistant from the pole of a mirror or lens. It is used to calculate the focal length.
definitions.example: The center of curvature of a convex lens is 20 cm from its pole.

definitions.definition: An imaginary line that passes through the pole and center of curvature of a mirror or lens. It is used to calculate the position of an image.
definitions.example: The principal axis of a convex lens is the line that passes through its pole and center of curvature.

definitions.definition: The distance between the object and the optical instrument. It is measured in meters.
definitions.example: The object distance of a microscope is 10 cm.

definitions.definition: The distance between the image and the optical instrument. It is measured in meters.
definitions.example: The image distance of a telescope is 20 cm.

definitions.definition: A measure of the ability of an optical instrument to collect light. It is defined as the sine of the half-angle of the cone of light that enters the instrument.
definitions.example: The numerical aperture of a microscope is 0.5.
